compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review advisory triage (verdict PASS + signed; 0 blocking). Re-run after remediating the first round (phasing, migration/rollback, callers-affected, file_impact set, What↔AC4 aligned). The second-round advisories, triaged:

- E2/F1 + T9 (where/how the coverage counter is surfaced, its lifecycle): FIXED IN DESIGN. The contract-violation report is surfaced at `verdict["coverage"]["verification_contract_violations"]` = `{malformed, duplicates:[idx], unexpected:[idx]}`, computed at decide-time, scoped to the single gate run (no cross-run state), and emitted ONLY when non-empty so the clean-path verdict stays byte-identical (attestation-safe). An ERROR log accompanies it.
- E1 (Phase 3 `require=` API as a deliverable): ACCEPTED — AC6 verifies the lever behaviorally (a require-mode resolve raises on an unresolved root), which necessarily exercises the new param; the API surface is implied by the test. No separate AC needed.
- T7 + E1 (Phase 4 "migration" — CI docs reference the shell check; old check removal not verified): SCOPE CLARIFIED. Phase 4 does NOT remove the CI shell gate; it ADDS a `compute_over_cap_modules()` function + a structural unit test (belt-and-suspenders). Nothing to migrate/remove, so no doc churn and no removal to verify. AC7 verifies the added structural assertion — correct as written.
- COH (sequencing vs independent-slice tension): MINOR. Phase 1 gates the PATTERN CHOICE only; each phase still ships as its own mergeable slice. Not contradictory.
- P6 (DET ac-quality heuristic flags compound 'and' criteria): ACKNOWLEDGED. The DET floor is a structural heuristic; the ACs are individually verifiable as written. Not re-splitting further (over-splitting harms readability) — justified, non-blocking.
- G5 (epic-scale: 4 phases / 8 ACs): JUSTIFIED as a single story. This is ONE cohesive contract-hardening change (the silent-degrade CLASS) shipped as one reviewable PR; fragmenting into an epic would scatter a single seam-contract design. User directed it as one story to execute now.

Net: no blocking issues; design unchanged except the explicit coverage-key spec above. Proceeding to claim against this signed PASS.

compiled_state.description:
Two gate defects this cycle (no_repo_root code-root drop; the prompted-structured verifier emitting an unschema'd shape that tolerant-parse silently dropped to empty → all findings `no-verification`) shared a root pattern: **a load-bearing contract between stages was implicit and only weakly enforced**, so a break degraded silently instead of failing loudly, and our tests could not catch it without brittle grep/glob/string heuristics. This story closes that CLASS for plan-review and the other LLM workflows.

## Why
- The Pass-2-verify → Pass-3-decide contract ("each finding gets a verification keyed by integer `index`") was implicit; a verifier returning a divergent shape produced an EMPTY-but-valid object (extra keys ignored at the Pydantic boundary), so the failure was invisible until findings mysteriously all went `no-verification`.
- The code-read-root contract (which snapshot a stage reads) was threaded ad-hoc across five hops and silently became `None` (the det-floor P2 `no_repo_root` abstain).
- Current gate self-tests lean on greps/globs/string-contains (e.g. the module-size allowlist↔doc lock-step, stale-path sweeps), which are brittle and miss structural breaks.
We want STRONG, EXPLICIT, TESTABLE contracts at the stage seams — so a break is a loud, deterministic TEST failure (and a loud runtime SIGNAL), not a silent degrade.

## Posture (user-directed): expand-contract — close the TESTING gap; do NOT change production outcomes
This story closes the testing gap WITHOUT changing the live gate's behavior. Production verdicts/decisions/findings/parse outcomes are UNCHANGED. The only runtime additions are additive OBSERVABILITY (a loud ERROR log + a coverage counter surfaced only WHEN a contract violation is detected — the clean path stays byte-identical, so attestation/signatures are unaffected). The strict enforcement (extra=forbid parse, distinct decide reason) is PINNED BY TESTS and documented as a one-line future flip; it is NOT enabled on the live path here.

## What (research-informed; authorized to research OSS). SEQUENCED — research gates the pattern; each seam is an independently-mergeable thin slice.

**Phase 1 — Research (gates the pattern).** Research popular, actively-maintained OSS that run multi-stage LLM/agent pipelines + structured-output contracts (candidates, not prescriptive: Pydantic AI, Instructor, Outlines/Guidance, LangGraph/LangChain runnable schemas, DSPy signatures, Marvin, BAML, Guardrails) for how they (a) make inter-stage contracts explicit, (b) fail loudly on contract drift, (c) test the seams without string heuristics. Capture findings + cited patterns in `docs/adr/`; record the adopted pattern BEFORE Phase-2 is finalized.

**Phase 2 — Pass-2→Pass-3 verification contract (thin slice, deterministic negative test).**
  * Provide a STRICT verification-contract validator (extra=forbid + required-`index`) that REJECTS a divergent shape (wrong wrapper key `findings`, wrong per-item key `attributes`, missing/non-int `index`) loudly — pinned by a deterministic test that also documents the current tolerant live behavior. NOT wired onto the live parse (expand-contract); a NOTE + the ADR document the one-line flip + back-out.
  * Replace the DUPLICATED inline silent-drop (kernel `merge_verifications_by_index` AND the workflow `plan_review_decide`) with ONE shared, structural reshape that returns the SAME tolerant map as today PLUS a contract-violation REPORT (malformed/no-`index` items, duplicate indices, out-of-range indices). In prod the report is LOGGED loudly (ERROR) + counted in verdict coverage (only when >0) — outcomes unchanged.
  * a deterministic NEGATIVE test that each break (wrong wrapper key, duplicate index, out-of-range index, malformed item) is detected structurally (no grep/glob/substring assertions).

**Phase 3 — code/ticket read-root contract.** Add an OPT-IN `require=` to `resolve_code_root` (raises a clear error when a stage that REQUIRES a root would resolve `None`) — NOT wired onto a live stage (no prod change). A deterministic test pins the #71 cascade (inside an active attested-snapshot context a stage resolves a NON-None root) AND the require-mode loud failure on an unresolved root (no string heuristics).

**Phase 4 — replace ONE brittle grep/glob self-check (advisory COH: AC4 floors at one).** Replace the module-size allowlist check (currently a shell `awk`/`comm`/`grep` in CI with no Python function or unit test) with a structural assertion: a `compute_over_cap_modules()` function + a unit test asserting the allowlist set's structural relationship to the computed over-cap set (containment/equality), not a doc-string lockstep.

### Migration / Rollback (advisory: pre-mortem on the tolerant→strict change)
Expand-contract by construction: prod stays tolerant (no outcome change); the strict path is test-pinned and flip-ready (a one-line `extra=forbid` enable + threading the distinct reason). Back-out of any added observability is a trivial revert. No live behavioral breaking change ships in this story.

### Callers affected (advisory: acknowledge consumers of the old behavior)
Consumers of the verifier→decide shape audited: kernel `verify_findings`/`merge_verifications_by_index` and the workflow `plan_review_decide` (the two reshape sites), `pass3_over_findings`/`pass3_decide` (decide), and the existing kernel tests that pin "no-index item silently dropped" / "raising chunk → INDETERMINATE" (`tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py`) — those tests are extended to also assert the new contract-violation REPORT while keeping the unchanged tolerant map.

## Scope
In: the strict contract validator + the shared structural reshape with a loud+counted report, the read-root `require` lever, the seam tests, the OSS research writeup, and migrating ONE high-value brittle grep/glob check. Out: flipping the live parse to strict; rewriting the workflow engine; non-LLM subsystems beyond the read-root contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An OSS-research writeup in `docs/adr/` cites how 3+ actively-maintained projects make LLM-pipeline/structured-output contracts explicit + testable, and records the patterns we adopt and why.
- [ ] A strict verification-contract validator REJECTS a wrong wrapper key / wrong per-item key / missing-or-non-int `index`, pinned by a deterministic test (the live parse stays tolerant — expand-contract).
- [ ] A single shared structural reshape replaces BOTH the kernel and the workflow inline silent-drop (no duplicated reshape remains) and returns the same tolerant map PLUS a contract-violation report.
- [ ] A duplicate / out-of-range / malformed verification index is detected and surfaced as a DISTINCT, loud signal in prod (an ERROR log + a verdict-coverage count surfaced only when >0) — distinct from a genuine `no-verification`; production decisions/verdict are unchanged.
- [ ] The verification-contract breaks above are covered by a deterministic negative test (no grep/glob/substring assertions).
- [ ] The code/ticket read-root contract is asserted by a deterministic test that fails if a stage resolves an unresolved/`None` root when one is required (no string heuristics).
- [ ] At least one brittle grep/glob self-check (the module-size allowlist) is replaced by a structural assertion computing the over-cap set from a function.
- [ ] No new test relies on grep/glob/substring-contains for a structural contract; `make check` + the suite pass.

file_impact.reason: strict verification model (extra=forbid, test-pinned) + shared structural reshape returning the tolerant map plus a contract-violation report; verify_findings surfaces + logs the report
pass3_over_findings/pass3_decide: optional distinct verifier-contract-violation reason path (test-pinned; not wired live)
export the new shared reshape + strict-validator helpers
plan_review_decide: replace the inline silent-drop with the shared reshape; ERROR-log + coverage-count violations (additive, no outcome change)
thread the contract-violation report through the plan-review pass3 wrapper + verdict coverage
resolve_code_root: opt-in require= lever that raises on an unresolved/None root (not wired live)
new ADR: OSS research on LLM-pipeline/structured-output contract enforcement + the patterns adopted
extend/add deterministic negative tests for the verification-contract reshape report + strict validator
deterministic test that the strict validator rejects divergent shapes while the tolerant path drops silently
deterministic read-root contract test (require-mode loud failure + #71 cascade resolves non-None)
new structural test: compute_over_cap_modules() vs the allowlist set (replaces the brittle shell grep/awk check)

compiled_state.title: Enforceable, testable contracts at LLM-workflow stage seams (kill the silent-degrade class)
